sales.title:
The Coin Cabinet - Ancients Auction 31, Lot 479 — BYZANTINE EMPIRE. Constantine IV 'Pogonatus'. 
Gold Solidus, dated IY 10 = AD 681/2. Carthage. 
Obv: CONAT N P (sic), helmeted and cuirassed bust facing slightly right, holding spear over shoulder and
sales.description:
BYZANTINE EMPIRE. Constantine IV 'Pogonatus'. 
Gold Solidus, dated IY 10 = AD 681/2. Carthage. 
Obv: CONAT N P (sic), helmeted and cuirassed bust facing slightly right, holding spear over shoulder and shield decorated with horseman motif; pellet in right field. Rev: ЧICTORIA AVG I (sic), cross potent in the form of a staurogram with base, set on three steps; I (Indictional year) in left field, globe in right field, CONOB (retrograde) in exergue.

In secure plastic holder, graded NGC Ch AU* Str...

sales.title: Stephen Album Rare Coins - Auction 54, Lot 93 — BYZANTINE EMPIRE: Constantine IV Pogonatus, 668-685, AV solidus (4.38g), Constantinople, ca. 674-681, SB-1154, with Heraclius and Tiberius, 2nd officina, helmeted, draped, and cuirassed bust facing, h
sales.description:
BYZANTINE EMPIRE: Constantine IV Pogonatus, 668-685, AV solidus (4.38g), Constantinople, ca. 674-681, SB-1154, with Heraclius and Tiberius, 2nd officina, helmeted, draped, and cuirassed bust facing, holding spear and shield // cross potent on three steps, Heraclius and Tiberius standing to the sides, some discoloration on reverse, edge marks, brilliant luster, Strike 3/5, Surface 3/5, NGC graded Mint State.

Estimate: 750 - 1000 USD...

sales.title: Stephen Album Rare Coins - Auction 54, Lot 95 — BYZANTINE EMPIRE: Constantine IV Pogonatus, 668-685, AV solidus (4.36g), Constantinople, ca. 674-681, SB-1154, with Heraclius and Tiberius, 6th officina, helmeted, draped, and cuirassed bust facing, h
sales.description:
BYZANTINE EMPIRE: Constantine IV Pogonatus, 668-685, AV solidus (4.36g), Constantinople, ca. 674-681, SB-1154, with Heraclius and Tiberius, 6th officina, helmeted, draped, and cuirassed bust facing, holding spear and shield // cross potent on three steps, Heraclius and Tiberius standing to the sides, slight clipping, faintly brushed, attractive luster, Strike 4/5, Surface 3/5, NGC graded AU.

Estimate: 600 - 800 USD...

sales.title: Stephen Album Rare Coins - Auction 54, Lot 96 — BYZANTINE EMPIRE: Constantine IV Pogonatus, 668-685, AV solidus (4.43g), Constantinople, ca. 674-681, SB-1154, with Heraclius and Tiberius, helmeted, draped, and cuirassed bust facing, holding spear a
sales.description:
BYZANTINE EMPIRE: Constantine IV Pogonatus, 668-685, AV solidus (4.43g), Constantinople, ca. 674-681, SB-1154, with Heraclius and Tiberius, helmeted, draped, and cuirassed bust facing, holding spear and shield // cross potent on three steps, Heraclius and Tiberius standing to the sides, officina mark largely off flan (likely B), lightly brushed, lustrous, Strike 4/5, Surface 4/5, NGC graded AU.

Estimate: 600 - 800 USD...
